settings.title: Settings
settings.gitConfigDesc: Set Git commit author info (repository level).
settings.phaseGateDesc: When enabled, AI must follow the writing workflow (prepare→outline→write→review→maintain) and cannot skip steps. When disabled, AI can use all tools freely.
settings.rebuildIndexDesc: If search is abnormal, rebuild the vector index for a specific novel.
settings.apiServerDesc: Expose HTTP API for external AI tools (e.g. MiMoCode). All tools callable via curl. Listens on 127.0.0.1 only.
settings.compressionThresholdDesc: Auto-compress when context usage reaches this ratio of model window.
settings.pleaseConfigureApiKey: Please configure at least one provider's API Key
settings.pleaseAddModel: Please add at least one model
settings.contextWindowPositive: Context window must be greater than 0
settings.maxOutputPositive: Max output must be greater than 0
settings.modelDefaultValues: Above are default values, please refer to provider documentation
settings.temperatureDesc: Controls the randomness of model output. Higher values are more creative, lower values are more deterministic. Range 0 ~ 2.

novel.importNeedsLLMDesc: Could not auto-detect chapter format. You can try AI analysis to identify chapters (only a few tokens consumed).

git.selectFileToDiff: Select a file to view diff
git.helpText1: After each conversation with AI, the system automatically records a version. Any manual edits you make between conversations are also saved before the next one starts.
git.helpText2: Here you can browse the complete creation history in chronological order and see what changed in each version.

skill.modeSmart: Smart — AI can invoke autonomously, user can also trigger via /
skill.modeCommand: Command — Only triggered by user via /, not shown in catalog
skill.modePermanent: Permanent — Auto-injected at session start, always active
skill.summaryPlaceholder: Brief description of this skill's function and trigger timing
skill.author: Author
skill.contributeDesc: Skills are AI's "professional knowledge cards". You can share your writing methodologies with the community and help more creators.
skill.contributeStep1: Fork the repository to your GitHub account
skill.contributeStep2: Create a new .md file in skills/ (based on the template)
skill.contributeStep3: Submit a Pull Request — it goes live after review

help.quickStart_welcomeDesc: Goink is a desktop AI novel writing assistant. It's more than a chatbot — it understands your novel's world, managing characters, locations, timelines, story arcs, and other creative elements, while assisting you with writing, reviewing, and brainstorming through AI conversations.
help.quickStart_leftBarDesc: Switch between functional panels: Search, Novel, Chapters, Preferences, Characters, Locations, Story Arcs, Timeline, Reader Perspective, Skills. Click an icon to enter the corresponding panel.
help.quickStart_centerContentDesc: Displays the main content of the current panel, such as chapter lists, character relation graphs, location network graphs, etc.
help.quickStart_rightChatDesc: The core area for AI conversations. You can have AI help you write, research, review, and manage creative elements here. AI will automatically invoke appropriate tools to fulfill your requests.
help.quickStart_bottomStatusDesc: Displays current operation status and file editing information.
help.quickStart_step1Desc: Create your first novel in the Novel panel.
help.quickStart_step2: Tell AI What You Want to Write
help.quickStart_step2Desc: Describe your ideas in the chat panel on the right. AI will automatically create characters, locations, chapters, and more as needed.
help.quickStart_step3Desc: AI helps you write, review, manage foreshadowing and story arcs. You can view and adjust everything in the left panel at any time.
help.quickStart_conceptToolsDesc: A set of operational capabilities that AI can invoke, such as reading chapters, creating characters, searching memory, etc. You can view the complete list in the "Tool Reference" tab.
help.quickStart_conceptSubagentsDesc: Independent AIs dedicated to specific tasks, such as memory retrieval and chapter review. They have their own context windows and won't interfere with the main conversation.
help.quickStart_conceptSkillsDesc: Predefined writing techniques and workflow templates that AI can invoke as needed. You can also create your own skills.
help.quickStart_conceptApprovalDesc: Switch between auto/manual approval at the bottom of the chat panel. In manual mode, AI's edit and delete operations require your confirmation before execution.
help.toolRef_intro: Below are all the tools available to AI, grouped by functional domain. When you make a request to AI in the chat, it will automatically select the appropriate tools to complete the task.
help.toolRef_get_chapter_list: Browse the chapter list of a novel, sorted by chapter number, with pagination support.
help.toolRef_read: Read the content of novel-related files, including chapter text, outlines, story status, and skill files.
help.toolRef_get_characters: View the list of all characters in the novel, with name search support.
help.toolRef_create_character: Create a new character in the novel, setting attributes like name, appearance, personality, etc.
help.toolRef_update_character: Modify an existing character's information, such as updating status or adding backstory.
help.toolRef_get_locations: View the list of all locations in the novel, supporting list, detail, and relation network modes.
help.toolRef_create_location: Create a new location in the novel, filling in name, description, type, etc.
help.toolRef_delete_record: Delete a specified record (characters, locations, timeline entries, etc.). Automatically checks associated data before deletion.
help.toolRef_get_preferences: View current creative preference settings, including writing style, narrative rules, etc.
help.toolRef_get_character_relations: View the relationship graph between characters, understanding their interactions and connections.
help.toolRef_get_timeline: View the novel's timeline, including foreshadowing, event entries, and chapter plans.
help.toolRef_get_story_arcs: View the overall structure of story arcs, understanding the progress of each plot line.
help.toolRef_get_reader_perspective: View the reader's cognitive state, tracking what the reader knows and wonders at different stages.
help.toolRef_search_story_memory: Use semantic search to find relevant information in the novel content, supporting natural language queries.
help.toolRef_create_preference: Add a new creative preference or writing rule.
help.toolRef_update_character_relationship: Edit or update the relationship between characters (friends, enemies, lovers, etc.).
help.toolRef_create_location_relation: Create spatial relationships between locations (adjacent, contains, etc.).
help.toolRef_create_timeline_entry: Create a new foreshadowing or timeline entry for planning plot development.
help.toolRef_update_timeline_entry: Modify an existing foreshadowing or timeline entry.
help.toolRef_update_chapter_plan: Update the three-layer plan slots (next / near / far) of a chapter, planning future writing direction.
help.toolRef_create_story_arc: Create a new story arc for tracking a complete plot line.
help.toolRef_update_story_arc: Modify a story arc's information and status.
help.toolRef_create_arc_node: Add a node to a story arc, marking a key plot turning point.
help.toolRef_update_arc_node: Modify information of a specific node in a story arc.
help.toolRef_create_reader_perspective_entry: Create a new reader perspective entry, defining what the reader knows and feels at a specific moment.
help.toolRef_update_reader_perspective_entry: Modify an existing reader perspective entry.
help.toolRef_edit: Edit novel files (chapters, outlines, story status, skills), supporting three modes: full replace, find & replace, and line range replace.
help.toolRef_run_subagent: Launch a specialized sub-agent for complex tasks (memory retrieval or chapter review). The sub-agent runs independently and returns a report.
help.toolRef_web_search: Search the web for real-world information, obtaining real-time data, news, or reference materials for writing.
help.toolRef_web_fetch: Fetch the main content of a specified webpage, returning cleaned pure text for reference.
help.subagent_whatIsDesc: Sub-agents are AI clones with independent context windows and dedicated tool sets. They focus on executing specific types of complex tasks and return result reports to the main conversation after completion. The main conversation context won't be filled with intermediate sub-agent processes, making sub-agents particularly suitable for tasks requiring extensive retrieval and multi-round analysis.
help.subagent_memoryDesc: A read-only sub-agent that can search in parallel across large amounts of story data, consolidating scattered character, location, timeline, and foreshadowing information into a coherent report. Ideal for scenarios requiring retrospective review of settings, finding hidden constraints, or cross-chapter information correlation.
help.subagent_memoryExample: For example: Ask AI to "Check whether all foreshadowing related to a certain character in chapters 3 through 5 has been resolved."
help.subagent_reviewDesc: A read-only sub-agent that performs multi-dimensional quality review on a specified chapter, checking character consistency, plot logic, foreshadowing management, reader perception, and story arc progression item by item, outputting a structured review report.
help.subagent_reviewExample: For example: Ask AI to "Review chapter 8 to see if the protagonist's personality is consistent with previous chapters."
help.subagent_howToUseDesc: Simply tell AI your needs in the chat, and AI will automatically determine whether to launch a sub-agent. For example: "Help me check if there are any contradictions with earlier settings in chapter 4" or "Help me organize all foreshadowing about Granny Wang." You can also explicitly ask AI to use a specific sub-agent for a task.
help.skill_whatIsDesc: Skills are predefined writing techniques or workflow templates. Each skill contains a specialized prompt — when AI invokes a skill, this prompt is temporarily added to the conversation context, guiding AI to follow a specific methodology to complete the task.
help.skill_builtinDesc: Basic writing skills that come with the app, such as brainstorming, character design, dialogue subtext, pacing control, etc., applicable to all novels.
help.skill_userLevelDesc: Skills you create in your user directory, applicable to all your novels. A user-level skill with the same name will override the built-in skill.
help.skill_novelLevelDesc: Skills that only take effect in the current novel, with the highest priority. Ideal for exclusive techniques customized for a specific project.
help.skill_howToUseDesc: Simply describe your needs in the chat, and AI will automatically determine whether to invoke a skill. You can also explicitly ask AI to use a specific skill, for example: "Use the brainstorming skill to help me come up with a few opening styles." The list of available skills can be viewed in the "Skills" panel in the left activity bar.
help.skill_howToCreateIntro: Skills are Markdown files containing a YAML front matter and body content. Creating a skill only requires:
help.skill_mdDescription: Briefly describe the purpose of this skill and when it's appropriate to invoke it
help.skill_mdBodyContent: Here is the detailed guidance content, telling AI what steps to follow, what style to adopt, and what points to pay attention to when executing this skill...
help.skill_howToCreateAfter: After creation, place the file in the user skills directory (you can see the path in the "Skills" panel on the left), or create it directly through AI's edit tool. The skills panel will automatically detect and load new files.
help.model_whyConfigDesc: Goink does not provide AI models itself. You need to connect to a third-party large model service to use AI features. Configuration is the process of telling Goink which provider's model to use and what the API Key is. The configuration entry is at Settings → Model Config.
help.model_builtinProviderDesc: The app comes with 5 mainstream providers: DeepSeek, GLM (Zhipu), MiniMax, MiMo (Xiaomi), and Kimi (Moonshot). Each built-in provider has an official model list — you just need to enter the API Key to use it. The API Key needs to be obtained by registering on the corresponding provider's website.
help.model_customProviderDesc: If your model service is compatible with the OpenAI API format (e.g., locally deployed Ollama, vLLM, or other third-party proxies), you can add a custom provider. You need to provide a name, Chat API URL, and API Key, then manually add or auto-discover models.
help.model_managementIntro: Each provider can be configured with multiple models. There are two ways to add them:
help.model_autoDiscoverDesc: Click the "Auto Discover" button, and Goink will call the provider's /models endpoint to list all available models. Check the models you want to import. Not all providers support this feature.
help.model_manualAddDesc: Click the "+ Add" button to manually fill in the model ID, name, context window size, max output length, and other information.
help.model_paramContextDesc: The maximum number of tokens a model can process at once, determining how long a conversation and materials the AI can "remember."
help.model_paramMaxOutputDesc: The maximum number of tokens in a single model response.
help.model_paramThinkingDesc: When enabled, the model performs internal reasoning before responding, suitable for complex creative tasks. Some models also support selecting reasoning levels (low / high / max).
help.model_paramVisionDesc: When enabled, the model can understand and analyze images.
help.model_temperatureDesc: Controls the randomness and creativity of model output. Range 0 ~ 2. Higher values produce more creative and unpredictable output; lower values produce more deterministic and conservative output. For creative writing, 0.7 ~ 1.0 is typically recommended; lower it when strict adherence to settings is needed. Note: For the Kimi provider, temperature is fixed internally by the model and the configured value does not take effect.
help.model_testStep1: 1. After entering the API Key and model, click the "Test" button to verify the connection is working. The test sends a minimal request and won't consume quota.
help.model_testStep2: 2. After the test passes, click "Save Config". All configuration (including API Key) will be encrypted and stored on the local disk.
help.model_testStep3: 3. After saving, you can see the configured models in the model selector at the top of the chat panel. Select one to use.
help.model_faqTestFailDesc: Check if the API Key is correct and the network is reachable. If the provider doesn't support auto-discovery, try manually adding the model.
help.model_faqApiKeySafeDesc: The API Key is stored on the local disk with AES-256 encryption (~/Goink/llm_config.enc). Note that this only protects against disk file scanning, not against physical device compromise.
help.model_faqMultipleProvidersDesc: Yes. All providers with API Keys filled in will be saved. Switch between them using the model selector in the chat.
help.context_whatIsDesc: Each time you chat with AI, Goink packages the conversation history and system prompts (System1/2/3) into a "context" sent to the model. Major model providers cache conversation prefixes — if the prefix remains unchanged across consecutive rounds, the model can directly reuse previous computation results (KV cache), skipping redundant processing, thereby reducing cost and latency. This is called a "cache hit."
help.context_cacheHitRateDesc: The status ring at the bottom of the chat panel shows the cache hit rate. A high hit rate (close to 100%) means most of the context is cached, resulting in lower costs and faster responses. A low hit rate means the model needs to recompute a large amount of content, which increases costs.
help.context_dropReasons: What Causes Cache Hit Rate to Drop
help.context_switchModelDesc: Switching to a different provider's model mid-conversation causes the cache to be recalculated from scratch. Since caching is provider-side, switching to a new provider means a brand new cache cycle, and the hit rate will temporarily drop to 0%. Even with different models from the same provider, some providers may clear the cache.
help.context_contextCompressDesc: Compression rebuilds the conversation prefix (generating a new summary, rebuilding System1/2/3), starting a new cache cycle. Although the hit rate drops in the short term, it controls token consumption — a necessary trade-off.
help.context_newSessionDesc: A new session builds context from scratch with no cache to reuse, so a 0% hit rate is normal. The hit rate will gradually increase as conversation rounds accumulate.
help.context_cacheTTLDesc: Provider caches are not permanent — if you don't use a session for a while, the provider may clear the cache. When you return to continue the conversation, the hit rate will start over. This is one of the reasons we recommend compressing after completing a phase of work (see practical tips below).
help.context_compressIntro: As the conversation progresses, historical messages accumulate and will eventually reach the model's context window limit (each model has a different window size). The compression mechanism retains the most recent messages, has AI generate a summary of older messages, and then replaces the old messages with the summary, freeing up space.
help.context_autoCompressDesc: Automatically triggered when token usage reaches 80% of the model's context window. No manual action needed — the system will automatically complete compression before the next conversation round. You'll see a "Compressing context" notification.
help.context_manualCompressDesc: There is a compress button on the status ring at the bottom of the chat panel. When you've completed a phase of work or the context is too lengthy, you can manually trigger compression to have AI continue based on a condensed summary. Manual compression takes effect immediately.
help.context_tip1Desc: If you do need to switch models, consider starting a new session to let the cache accumulate from the new provider.
help.context_tip2Label: Compress or start a new session after completing a phase of work
help.context_tip2Desc: When you finish a chapter, complete a review round, or reach other milestones, you can manually compress or start a new session to prevent the context from becoming increasingly bloated.
help.context_tip3Label: Compress manually before stepping away for a while
help.context_tip3Desc: Provider caches have a time limit — the cache may be cleared after a period of inactivity. If the current token count is already high, rather than returning to face high tokens + zero cache, it's better to compress before leaving and continue directly on the new summary when you return.
help.context_tip4Label: Regular compression in long conversations is normal
help.context_tip4Desc: A temporary drop in hit rate is exchanged for lower token consumption and faster responses.
help.approval_intro: Goink supports both "AI autonomous creation" and "fine-grained collaborative approval" modes. You don't need to choose one — switch at any time from the bottom of the chat panel to suit different stages of your creative process.
help.approval_autoModeDesc: AI executes all operations directly — creating characters, editing chapters, deleting records... everything is done automatically, you just watch. Ideal for rapid progress, moments of inspiration, or if you prefer a "check after" rather than "approve before" workflow.
help.approval_manualModeIntro: Every write operation AI proposes — editing chapters, deleting records, etc. — will be paused first and embedded in the chat flow as an approval card, awaiting your decision.
help.approval_rejectDesc: Don't execute this operation; AI adjusts direction based on your feedback.
help.approval_modifyApproveDesc: Manually adjust the content based on AI's edit, then confirm execution. This capability is especially crucial for chapter writing — AI produces the first draft, you refine it and submit directly.
help.approval_manualModeNote: Ideal for scenarios requiring high setting consistency, where you don't want AI making unauthorized changes, such as key chapter writing, character setting modifications, deletion operations, etc. In this mode, you always have the final say.
help.approval_howToSwitchDesc: There is an "Auto" button in the toolbar at the bottom of the chat panel. Click to toggle between manual approval and auto mode. The switch takes effect immediately — you can change modes at any time during a conversation.

profile.pastYearStats_one: Past year · {{count}} day with writing records
profile.pastYearStats_other: Past year · {{count}} days with writing records
profile.noWritingRecord: No writing records yet. Start writing — every day's word count will be recorded.
